title: kbu-beril-worktree — parallel BERIL sessions via git worktrees, managed by the kbu CLI
status_note: Third kbu↔BERIL augmentation PRD. One repo (KBUtilLib only); no BERIL change.
confront_round_1: task-cd4747d6 (codex/h100, 2026-06-16, artifact 3d0432df). 15 stalls + 7 free critiques; ZERO structural issues (all precision gaps: exact paths, ID charset, output schema, exit codes, argv/env contract). Folded all 15 into Implementation Decisions + 23 Acceptance Criteria; adopted 1 free critique as binding (doctor checks symlink targets readable). One correction to adversary rec #8: replicate run_start's exact Vertex env-key mapping, NOT os.environ.update(get_vertex_config()) (that dict is config, not env vars). No drops.

foundational_decisions.core_insight: git worktrees share ONE .git; the projects/<id> branch lives in primary BERIL git the whole time, so there is NO merge-back. rm removes the worktree DIRECTORY ONLY and KEEPS the branch.
foundational_decisions.home: kbu beril worktree {new,open,start,rm,ls,set-root,doctor} — nested click group under existing beril_cmd in src/kbutillib/cli/beril.py; thin CLI over a new kbutillib/beril_worktree/ library package (mirrors kbutillib/harness/).
foundational_decisions.rm_semantics: git worktree remove dir only; NEVER git branch -d; git worktree prune for stale entries; idempotent no-op if absent; refuse if dirty unless --force.
foundational_decisions.recreate: new adopts an existing projects/<id> branch (no -b) or creates it off main; branching off the main REF works even though the primary checkout is parked on a project branch.
foundational_decisions.merge_to_main: out of scope for kbu; recreate worktree + open Claude + /submit (BERIL reviewed-PR gate). kbu never touches main.
foundational_decisions.symlinks: every new symlinks .env -> beril_root/.env and .venv-berdl -> beril_root/.venv-berdl; both gitignored; one place to refresh token, no venv re-bootstrap.
foundational_decisions.cursor_shape: Shape B — one Cursor window per worktree; workspace file at <worktree_root>/<id>.code-workspace OUTSIDE the git tree (BERIL stays pristine); mirrors BERIL.code-workspace settings; ${workspaceFolder}/.venv-berdl/bin/python resolves via symlink.
foundational_decisions.launch_split: open <id> = cursor (Claude extension, Chris); start <id> = CLI-agent proxy (JupyterHub users).
foundational_decisions.launch_proxy: FULL kbu proxy, ONE repo (Chris chose this over a BERIL-side guard to avoid shared-repo PR friction). DRIFT MITIGATION (binding): import beril_cli helpers (get_default_agent, get_vertex_config from beril_cli.config; _sync_auth_token from beril_cli.start); replicate ONLY run_start orchestration shell minus _checkout_release (chdir, sync token, vertex env, opus default, /berdl_start onboard, os.execvp). Do NOT copy helper bodies. Factor pre-execvp assembly into a pure (binary, argv, env) function for testing.
foundational_decisions.drift_tripwire: kbu beril worktree doctor + a unit test assert beril_cli + the 3 borrowed symbols import; fail loud naming the missing symbol on a BERIL rename.
foundational_decisions.config: kbu's OWN config at ~/.kbutillib/config.yaml (via SharedEnvUtils), a `beril` section {root, worktree_root}. Resolution: beril_root = --beril-root > BERIL_ROOT > config > error; worktree_root = --root > WORKING_BERIL_DIRECTORY > config > default <beril_root>/../WorkingBERIL. Chris worktree_root = ~/Dropbox/Projects/WorkingBERIL/. Never write BERIL's config.
foundational_decisions.hazard: beril start inside a worktree runs unconditional _checkout_release (git checkout release-tag) -> detaches worktree off projects/<id>. Documented; use kbu beril worktree start instead.

tests: manager core against scratch temp git repo (new/adopt/rm-keeps-branch/idempotent-rm/dirty-refusal/force/symlinks-gitignored/workspace-file-outside-tree)
config resolution precedence (flag>env>config>default) + set-root persists to ~/.kbutillib/config.yaml not BERIL config
launch proxy import tripwire + argv/env assembly (opus default, /berdl_start onboard, --skip-onboard, no checkout)
doctor matrix (beril_cli importable/not; each borrowed symbol present/renamed)
out_of_scope: merging project branches to main (/submit owns it)
constraints: worker IS Claude (Max plan) — no Anthropic API / no subprocess claude; proxy uses os.execvp to hand off the terminal, not a model call
one repo: KBUtilLib only, no BERIL PR
